Metro Council WA Local Government / Perth WA

Your elected council

Have your say.

Council meetings, elected members, open consultations, and the decisions being made on your behalf.

Open consultations

Three things council wants to hear from you about.

Council decisions are better when residents are part of them. Each consultation has multiple ways to take part — written submissions, online surveys, in-person forums.

Open

Residential density code review.

The state government has released new density guidelines. Council is consulting on how they apply locally — what's protected, what changes, where transition zones sit.

Closes 15 May Take part →
Open

Food waste trial — phase 2 expansion.

Should the food waste collection trial expand to 2,000 households? We want feedback from residents in the proposed expansion zones.

Closes 22 April Take part →
Open

Park upgrade priorities — annual survey.

$2.4M for park upgrades next financial year. Tell us where it should go — playgrounds, dog parks, tree planting, sport facilities, accessibility.

Closes 30 April Take part →

Council meetings

How council decisions get made.

Past minutes
Upcoming

Ordinary council meeting.

Tuesday 22 April, 6:00pm at the Civic Centre. Public gallery seats 60. Public question time 6:00–6:15pm. Agenda available 7 days prior.

22 April / 6:00pm Agenda →
Upcoming

Planning committee.

Wednesday 23 April, 5:30pm at the Civic Centre. Major development applications. Speakers must register 24 hours in advance.

23 April / 5:30pm Agenda →
Information

How to address council.

Any resident can speak at public question time or apply to make a deputation on a specific agenda item. Plain language guide available.

Updated quarterly Read guide →

Want to talk to your councillor?

Twelve elected councillors across four wards. Each councillor publishes their contact details and holds monthly community catch-ups. Find yours by suburb on the elected members directory.

Find your ward

Metro Council WA

Delivering local government services to 85,000 residents across 12 suburbs in inner and middle Perth.

Metro Council WA acknowledges the Whadjuk people of the Noongar nation as the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work, and we pay our respects to Elders past and present.
